---
title: "Data Types for GIS" 
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

Focus more on data - Flush out what we want the end product to look like

Annotation vs geospatial data - importing rather than sketching layers -
Raster/DEM - Lat/Long CSV file - uploading your won dataset and/or
finding a dataset in living atlas

Features have attributes - a data table/tabular data - columns have
fixed data types (a lot of them) - for example bikes

Types of data:

-   Vector: Difference between point, line, and polygon
-   Raster: an array of values that can be used
    -   Single band
    -   Multibands

How to add a new data layer: - Import point data from a csv, lat/long
csv file - Raster/DEM - Importing or uploading a dataset vs finding a
good dataset in living atlas

-   Facilities data (buildings)
-   DEM
-   Bike paths
-   Trees

Esri ArcGIS Credit Management Policy:

-   Monitor your credits: All ArcGIS Online accounts created under the
    UCSB site license are allocated an initial balance of **1000 service
    credits**. Regularly check your credit balance through your user
    profile.

    -   Storage credits: depend on data size and how long content is
        stored per month. Most commonly hosted feature layers like
        points, lines, polygons and tables.

    -   Transactions: credits used for specific transaction-based tools
        in ArcGIS Online. These include feature and imagery analysis,
        geocoding, or using premium content from ArcGIS Living Atlas.

-   Manage your content: Periodically review and delete unnecessary
    content to reduce storage costs.

-   Data Stewardship: ArcGIS Online is a powerful platform for data
    analysis and visualization but should not be considered a permanent,
    long-term data archive. All the analysis in our workshops aren't
    costing us a significant amount of credits.

-   For more information, visit Esri's [Understand
    Credits](https://doc.arcgis.com/en/arcgis-online/administer/credits.htm)
    Page.

-   ***Stop saving global .tiff files!***
